title: Specify slice synthesis response
description: Validates the agent-led synthesis step's response. Synthesis is always agent-dispatched: there is no tool consumer, so there is no closed *request* wire shape — `specify slice synthesize` assembles the step's inputs directly (the slice's inline Evidence and the bound target's shape brief, which the CLI resolves from `target` and provides to the step) and schema-validates only the returned response. The response (`kind: response`) carries the `model` validated against the single `model.schema.json` (claims, agreement, prose; the agent omits kernel-owned and header fields, which are optional in that schema) plus prose-only Markdown artifacts (requirement bodies without `ID:` / `Sources:` / `Status:` lines). The engine validates the response, then projects the kernel — re-deriving `requirements[].id`, `.status`, `claims[].winner`, rendered source lists and provenance, ignoring any kernel-owned fields the agent supplied (normalize, never reject), and resolving authority from the on-disk Evidence and any per-slice override — stamps the `version` / `slice` / `project` header, re-validates the merged `model.yaml` against the same schema, renders provenance lines into `spec.md`, then persists. `model.schema.json` and `synthesis.schema.json` are registered together so the relative `$ref` resolves. Closed shape — unknown fields are rejected.
